extra.message: User-controlled input from the 'name' query parameter is concatenated into the 'content' string (line 38) and then passed to Jinja2's Template() and rendered on line 41. Because the user input becomes part of the template source itself, an attacker can inject Jinja2 template directives (e.g. {{7*7}} or {{ self.__init__.__globals__ }}) leading to Server-Side Template Injection and potential Remote Code Execution.

cwe: CWE-94: Improper Control of Generation of Code ('Code Injection')

extra.message: User-controlled input from the 'name' query parameter is embedded directly into an HTML response via an f-string without any HTML escaping, and the endpoint returns HTMLResponse. An attacker can inject arbitrary HTML/JavaScript (e.g. ?name=<script>alert(1)</script>) resulting in reflected Cross-Site Scripting.

cwe: CWE-79: Improper Neutralization of Input During Web Page Generation ('Cross-site Scripting')

extra.message: A secret token ('SUPER_SECRET_TOKEN = "5u93R53Cr3tT0k3n"') is hardcoded directly in source code. Hardcoded credentials/secrets stored in source are a known weakness and can be extracted from the repository.
A value labelled 'SUPER_SECRET_NAME' is hardcoded in source code instead of being retrieved from the environment (the FIXME comment confirms the value was intended to be secret).

The FastAPI application is configured with debug=config.DEBUG which is driven by an environment variable (DEBUG). If enabled in production, verbose debug output and tracebacks may leak sensitive information (stack traces, environment, source paths) to remote users.

requests.get() is invoked with config.PUBLIC_IP_SERVICE_URL, which is loaded from an environment variable without validation. Although not directly user-controllable via HTTP, if the deployment environment (or any process that can set env vars) is attacker-influenced, the service will issue outbound HTTP requests to attacker-chosen URLs and reflect the body back into the HTML response (line 36/38), enabling SSRF/content-reflection risks.
